description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages download, build:rpm:stage1, build:rpm:stage2, build:rpm:stage3, build:rpm:stage4, build:rpm:stage5, build:rpm:stage6, build:rpm:stage7, build:web and deploy, using the variables GPG_FILE_NAME, GPG_KEY_ID, GPG_NAME, LC_ALL, REPO_URL_BASE, base, download1, download1_77, download3, download4, stage1_72, stage1_77_python34, stage2_72, stage2_77, stage2_77_python36, stage3_77, stage4_77, stage4_77_cmake3, stage5_77, stage5_77_spirv, stage6_77, stage7_77. In the download stage add a job named download that runs on image centos:7, executes: yum install -y epel-release; yumdownloader -q $download1 --destdir=download1; yumdownloader -q --source $download1 --destdir=src_download1; yumdownloader -q $download1_77 --destdir=download1_77; yumdownloader -q --source $download1_77 --destdir=src_download1_77; yumdownloader -q $download3 --destdir=download3; yumdownloader -q --source $download3 --destdir=src_download3; yumdownloader -q $download4 --destdir=download4; yumdownloader -q --source $download4 --destdir=src_download4; yumdownloader -q --source $stage1_72 --destdir=stage1_72; yumdownloader -q --source $stage1_77_python34 --destdir=stage1_77_python34; yumdownloader -q --source $stage2_72 --destdir=stage2_72; yumdownloader -q --source $stage2_77 --destdir=stage2_77; yumdownloader -q --source $stage2_77_python36 --destdir=stage2_77_python36; yumdownloader -q --source $stage3_77 --destdir=stage3_77; yumdownloader -q --source $stage4_77 --destdir=stage4_77; yumdownloader -q --source $stage4_77_cmake3 --destdir=stage4_77_cmake3; yumdownloader -q --source $stage5_77 --destdir=stage5_77; yumdownloader -q --source $stage5_77_spirv --destdir=stage5_77_spirv; yumdownloader -q --source $stage6_77 --destdir=stage6_77; yumdownloader -q --source $stage7_77 --destdir=stage7_77, and stores paths stage*, download*, src_download* as artifacts. In each RPM build stage add jobs named build:rpm:stageX:centos7.Y:flavor (using image harbottle/centos7i386:7.Yrpmbuild) that run a series of yum -y install commands: first "yum -y install ${base}", then install the appropriate downloaded RPMs (e.g. ./download1/*.rpm, ./download1_77/*.rpm, ./stage1_*/rpmbuild/RPMS/*/*.rpm, ./stage2_*/rpmbuild/RPMS/*/*.rpm, ./download3/*.rpm, ./stage3_*/rpmbuild/RPMS/*/*.rpm, ./download4/*.rpm, ./stage4_*/rpmbuild/RPMS/*/*.rpm, ./stage5_*/rpmbuild/RPMS/*/*.rpm, ./stage6_*/rpmbuild/RPMS/*/*.rpm) as listed per job, optionally run patch_srpm.sh with the given spec and build flags (e.g. for stage1:centos7.7:python34 run ". ./patch_srpm.sh stage1_77_python34/python34*.src.rpm python34.spec 'run_selftest_suite 1' 'run_selftest_suite 0'"; for stage5:centos7.7:spirv run ". ./patch_srpm.sh stage5_77_spirv/spirv-tools*.src.rpm spirv-tools.spec 'cmake3 -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release' 'cmake3 -DSPIRV_WERROR=OFF -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release'"; for stage7 run two patch_srpm.sh commands with wine spec conditions), then source the rebuild script (e.g. ". ./rebuild_rpm.sh stage1_72", "./rebuild_rpm.sh stage2_77", "./rebuild_rpm.sh stage5_77_spirv" etc., adding flags like "--without tests --without optimizations" or "--without test" where specified), and publish the generated stage* directories as artifacts. In the build:web stage add a job named build:web using image centos:7 that runs: yum -y install https://harbottle.gitlab.io/harbottle-main/7/x86_64/harbottle-main-release.rpm; yum -y install epel-release ergel-release; yum -y install rubygem-filesize nodejs-bower rpm-build expect createrepo rpmdevtools rpm-sign; . ./build_release_rpm.sh; mkdir -p public/7/{i386,SRPMS}; mv -f stage*/rpmbuild/RPMS/*/*.rpm public/7/i386 || true; mv -f download*/*.rpm public/7/i386 || true; mv -f *release*.rpm public/7/i386 || true; mv -f stage*/*.src.rpm public/7/SRPMS || true; mv -f stage*/rpmbuild/SRPMS/*.src.rpm public/7/SRPMS || true; mv -f src_download*/*.src.rpm public/7/SRPMS || true; mv -f release/SRPMS/*.src.rpm public/7/SRPMS || true; . ./sign_rpm.sh public/7/{i386,SRPMS}/*.rpm; createrepo public/7/i386; createrepo public/7/SRPMS; . ./build_web.sh, and publishes the public directory as an artifact. Finally add a pages job using image ruby:2.3 that runs "pwd", is manual (when: manual), and publishes the public directory as an artifact for deployment.
